title: Agropyron divergens
taxonomy: Agropyron divergens is classified as a species of grass within the genus Agropyron, which belongs to the family Poaceae. The genus Agropyron is a group of cool-season grasses commonly found in temperate regions. Agropyron divergens is a perennial grass that is native to Europe and Asia, and is also known as creeping soft brome or creeping brome. It is closely related to other species within the genus, including Agropyron repens and Agropyron junceum. Agropyron divergens is often used as a forage crop or for soil stabilization in certain regions.
uses: Agropyron divergens is also known as slender wheatgrass or bluebunch wheatgrass, but information about its medicinal uses was not found.
